source.title: Strategy excerpt
source.text: Enterprise onboarding takes six weeks on average right now. Sales reports that enterprise onboarding is the top blocker for expansion deals this quarter. The team agreed to cut onboarding to two weeks by Q3. One open question is whether self-serve onboarding can replace the manual setup steps without losing the white-glove feel.

spans.exact: enterprise onboarding is the top blocker for expansion deals
cut onboarding to two weeks by Q3
self-serve onboarding can replace the manual setup steps
a sentence that no longer exists in this source

briefing_turns.speaker: agent
briefing_turns.text: Sales is flagging onboarding as the thing standing between you and expansion. I want to mark that as a risk.

briefing_turns.speaker: agent
briefing_turns.text: The team already committed to a target here: onboarding down to two weeks by Q3. That reads like a decision worth keeping.

briefing_turns.speaker: agent
briefing_turns.text: There is still an open question about whether self-serve can carry the load without losing the white-glove feel.

artifacts.text: Enterprise onboarding friction is the top blocker for expansion deals.
